title: The Victory at Van Don
summary: The young general Tran Khanh Du cleverly defeated the enemy's supply fleet, leaving them in a state of hunger and desperation.

slides.text: Did you know? For a large army to win, they must have full stomachs. The Mongol-Yuan army had a fleet of ships carrying food following behind them.
slides.imagePrompt: Large transport ships heavily loaded with rice bags and barrels, slow moving on the sea, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: General Tran Khanh Du recognized this weakness. He led our troops to ambush them in the beautiful sea region of Van Don.
slides.imagePrompt: Small Vietnamese boats hidden behind rocky islands in Ha Long bay style, waiting for the enemy, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: As the supply fleet passed, our troops suddenly launched a fierce attack. All of the enemy's food supplies were sunk into the sea or destroyed.
slides.imagePrompt: Sea battle, fire on the transport ships, bags of rice falling into the water, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Losing their food, the enemy soldiers panicked and became exhausted. This was a crucial turning point that helped our troops achieve the great victory later.
slides.imagePrompt: Hungry enemy soldiers looking at the empty sea, stomachs growling (visual effect), children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: Who commanded the victory at Van Don?
What was the main goal of our troops in the Battle of Van Don?
options: To attack the enemy's food supply fleet
slides.answer: To attack the enemy's food supply fleet
